title: "app auth sso setup"
command: "teams app auth sso setup"
---

<!-- AUTOGENERATED:START - Do not edit between these markers -->
# teams app auth sso setup

Set up SSO for an Azure bot (configures AAD app + OAuth connection + manifest)

## Usage

```
teams app auth sso setup [appId] [options]
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description | Required |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| `appId` | App ID | No |

## Options

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--connection-name <name>` | [OPTIONAL] OAuth connection name (default: sso) |
| `--scopes <scopes>` | [OPTIONAL] Scopes (default: User.Read) |
| `--client-secret <secret>` | AAD app client secret |
<!-- AUTOGENERATED:END -->

## Details

Configures Single Sign-On (SSO) for a Teams bot in a single command. This is one of the most complex operations the CLI handles — it touches AAD app configuration, Azure Bot OAuth settings, and the Teams manifest.

### What It Configures

**Step 1: AAD App** (via Microsoft Graph)
- Sets identifier URI to `api://botid-{botId}`
- Creates `access_as_user` delegated scope
- Pre-authorizes Teams desktop and web clients
- Adds Bot Framework redirect URI

**Step 2: Azure Bot OAuth Connection** (via `az` CLI)
- Creates an Aadv2 OAuth connection on the bot
- Links it to the AAD app with `tokenExchangeUrl`

**Step 3: Teams Manifest** (via TDP API)
- Sets `webApplicationInfo.id` and `webApplicationInfo.resource`
- Adds `token.botframework.com` to `validDomains`

### Prerequisites

- Bot must be in **Azure** (not BF tenant). If it's in BF, migrate first: `teams app bot migrate`
- Azure CLI (`az`) installed and logged in
- You need the app's client secret (pass via `--client-secret`)

### Examples

Basic setup with default connection name (`sso`) and scopes (`User.Read`):

```bash
teams app auth sso setup <appId> --client-secret <secret>
```

Custom connection name and scopes:

```bash
teams app auth sso setup <appId> --client-secret <secret> --connection-name my-sso --scopes "User.Read Mail.Read"
```

### Verifying

After setup, run diagnostics to confirm everything is wired correctly:

```bash
teams app doctor <appId>
```

See [SSO Architecture](/concepts/sso-architecture) for a deep dive on how SSO works.
